metadata.title: Dutch Real Estate Social Media Landscape
metadata.methodology: Manual audit of Dutch RE-related social accounts across TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube. Follower counts and view averages captured during Q1 2026 audit window.

market_size.note: NVM is the dominant trade association; ~50% of active firms are NVM-affiliated

social_adoption.definition_of_meaningful: Active account with 1,000+ followers and at least 1 post per week over the past 3 months
social_adoption.youtube_adoption: Negligible. Top RE channel (Digimakelaars) has only 6,870 subscribers.
social_adoption.instagram_adoption: Higher raw account count but low video-first usage; most accounts are static-image listing posts

top_accounts.notes: Highest follower count in Dutch RE TikTok
Highest avg views in the dataset. Fundaparels format (mocking unusual Funda listings) drives massive engagement. Entertainment-first, expertise second.
Instagram-primary. Luxury Amsterdam segment. Not active on TikTok.
Franchise model — multiple agents contribute content under one brand
Education-first content focused on mortgage process and buyer guidance
Strongest growth trajectory in the dataset. 118% follower growth over 6 months. Regional market (Apeldoorn).
Fix-and-flip niche. Single video hit 787K views — demonstrates viral potential of transformation content even with small following.

tiktok.algorithm_advantage: Discovery-based feed; content served to non-followers based on watch behavior

instagram.algorithm_advantage: Follower-weighted feed; Reels have some discovery but lower than TikTok

youtube.algorithm_advantage: Search-driven discovery; longer shelf life per video
youtube.note: YouTube RE adoption in NL is minimal. Even the top channel has under 7K subscribers.

platform_comparison.note: TikTok engagement rate is ~13x Instagram for RE content (8.5% vs 0.65%)

personality_vs_company.insight: Individual personalities (face-to-camera, personal brand) outperform company/brand accounts by approximately 4-5x on average views per video
personality_vs_company.implication: AI-generated content that features a human face/personality element will likely outperform purely property-focused content

posting_frequency.correlation: Accounts posting 4+ times per week show significantly higher follower growth than those posting 1-2 times per week
posting_frequency.implication_for_reelforge: AI-assisted production can help agents maintain 4-6x/week cadence without proportional time investment

posts_analyzed_basis.methodology: ~50 accounts with meaningful TikTok presence, posting an average of 4 videos/week, observed over a 12-week audit window (Q1 2026)
posts_analyzed_basis.note: Conservative estimate. Some accounts post 6+/week, which would increase the total. 2,400 is a defensible lower bound.
